text: The Lands of
Wraidorian
A campaign setting for gameplay in the D&D 2024 system
Players:  Spencer as Goofer Goober (Rock!)
Ben as Jan  Martin as Tal Bawa
Game Master:  Matthew Brown
Real-world timing:  October 09, 2025 - Ongoing
Background

In the lands of Wraidorian, numbers carry the breath  of divinity itself. As such, the Sacred Nine weaves  through every aspect of existence, of sentience. The  Nine winds through the mystical foundation upon  which reality rests. The Sacred Nine represents  completion, harmony, and cosmic balance. It is perfection  through unity, as once nine great empires ruled the  land, nine colleges of great magic preserved wisdom  and furthered science, and the nine levels of magic  stepped toward enlightenment.
History

We live today in the Ninth Age, an age of peace and  generalized prosperity. Throughout time, Wraidorian  has been host to the rise and fall of good, evil, magic,  and life itself. Time saw the development of Races, and  from them Subraces and mixed races, and from them  entire cultures, civilizations, empires and communities.  Collectively, the beings of Wraidorian became known  as the Sentient.

Three holds deified power as the Foundation of the  Nine. As such, the number three is the representation  of growth, creation, and the trilogy of mind, body, and  spirit. Eleven holds as the number of pure potential, the  Divine Prime, a numerological representation of the  sacred unknown and mysteries yet to unfold.
Ages of Wraidorian
1.​ The Dispersion of Civilization: The birth of
The chronology of Wraidorian follows the Hallowed  Numerology, with eleven months in one year, three  weeks in one month, and nine days per week.
sentience and mortality.
2.​ The Founding of Magic: Sentience gave way
to exploration and creativity, and as such,  Magic interwoven itself into Wraidorian.
Civilization & Culture
3.​ Hubris of Makador (The Age of Darkness):

The races of D&D live largely in unity, though  bastions of monocultures do exist where some races  thrive more than others, such as dwarven  under-mountain cities, or elvish villages of the trees, or  Dragonborn volcano towns, or Tiefling canyon  communes. Halflings tend to either co-locate with their  clans either in prairies and grasslands, or find their way  to every market in the empire. The rare races disperse  themselves betwixt the others, to no one’s complaint.
A great evil swept over the land, as well as  famines, plagues, and war.
4.​ Cities of the Skies: Out of the vanquishing of
Makador came the most powerful magic to  have graced the Sentient. Knowledge,  architecture, and magecraft advanced  rapidly.
5.​ The Collapse (The Second Age of
Magic in Daily Life
Darkness/The Age of Dusk): A powerful  mage took too much magic for himself,  allegedly going beyond the Ninth, and  collapsed the Sentient into ruin for a  millennia.

Most citizens know a cantrip or perhaps two, with  base magic being as common as humming a tune. There  are those which magic fully avoids, who are pitied  similarly to those who cannot carry such a melody. In a  parallel, those with great power are celebrated and  watched carefully, much like the most talented of  performers.
6.​ The Ninth Age: The current age. Magic is
granted to sentient folks with some  regularity, with about half of folks knowing a  cantrip, though leveled spellcasting is quite  rare.
Blessings of Wraidorian
“Nine Paths converge upon your doorstep” (Opportunity)  “From Nine winds, may clarity find you” (Indecision)  “Thrice blessed, thrice protected” (Journeys)  “Three roots of one tree” (Partnership)

You walk through the world of Wraidorian, a realm  wherein magic flows like song and the gods guide  history with gentle hands. You find yourselves within  the Ninth Age, specifically 232 NA. This is an era of  relative peace wherein knights and archers defend the  land, while mages remain rare treasures on the  battlefield.
Superstitions
Counting to nine before important decisions
Hallowed Numerology
Avoiding groups of precisely six
Arranging items in sets of three for luck
Knocking three times before entry
“May the Nine guide your path” - Common  Wraidorian blessing of stability and safety.
Empires
closely with owning the magic and beauty of the cliffs  and falls.

Halcyon is very much a melting pot of the emperor.  Many see it as a bastion of hope, a place to find  themselves anew in its crystalline cliffs and waters.  Lorded over by the stalwart, stubborn, heirless King  Appoleas Apollyean III, a human of 53 years, and by his  side Evytte, his regal, fancy queen. The king and queen  take a hands-on approach to Halcyon, often hearing the  pleas of citizens and immigrants alike in their court.  When they are unable, the Crystal Council will hear  them instead. When there is trouble, Aylin’s Stalwarts  will take care to clean it up. Named after their founding  member who lost his life defending the cliffside from  trolls.
Apollyean

The Apollyean Empire stands as the preeminent  power in Wraidorian during the Ninth Age, positioning  itself as the beacon of stability following the  catastrophic Collapse. Its identity is built upon an  inherent tension: the desire for the control of ancient  magic that its capital sits upon, balanced against the  political necessity of secular, hands-on governance.
Helion
A nomadic empire of desert-dwelling humans and  Tieflings known for their powerful mounted cavalry,  trade routes, and reverence for the sun and stars.
Districts of Halcyon

• The Igneous: (Pop. 3k) The noble sector which  houses the castle and the aristocracy. Only district  with torches.    • The Sedimentary: (Pop. 6k) The home of lifelong  Halcyons and established families. The district looks  nearly empty at night.    • The Metamorphic: (Pop. 11k) Bustling district of  immigrants, traders, and newcomers
Geography
We in the northern hemisphere, temperate (think  Ottawa).
Notable Cities & Towns

A hub of trade and commerce, little is actually  produced from within the boundaries of Halcyon by  way of goods. Food is mainly delivered through the  trade routes along the cliffs, which Halcyon can afford  with its moderately vast wealth and power. As the  capital city, swathes of gold are funneled in by means of  taxes and political gifts, along with tourism and  commerce.
Halcyon
Witio-Blessed mines are nearby.
Life is largely good within Halcyon, though trouble  may brew where the reflections cannot touch.

Halcyon is the capital of the empire of Apollyean. A  wall-less city of 80,000, Halcyon sits beside the Crystal  Falls, on the shore of the Lake of the Ancestors, and at  the start of the Mirrorbrook. A common saying of  Halcyon is to see your forebears in your own reflection.  This may be in part due to the locality of the  Reflections’ Crest, the cliff face that is the end of the  El’drath’el Range. In this cliff, it's said one may see   spirits floating through the throughways and streets.  One day's walk from the ocean, it’s said that Halcyon is  there because it is the last navigable point sailing  upstream on the Mirrorbrook. Many believe, though,  that the Apollyean empire had motives aligned more
Haybrook

Imperium is a military dictatorship led by the Emerald  Emperor himself who reigns with an iron first and  cult-like status. The town produces high quality steel  procured from the mines that surround the city in many  directions. Its relation between other cities is stone  cold, only meeting with foreigners for export and  import. Citizens' lives are repeats of the same thing  everyday, wake up, do your job, in your home by  nightfall. The Imperium is under strict military control  enforced by the military police. Curfew is by sun set.  People either obey out of fear or out of admiration for  the emperor.  The law is strictly enforced, and most  crimes either lead to death by hanging or sentenced to  the prison labour camps located by the mines. The  military is large and there are two cores, the military  police and the military convoy. The military police  patrol the city, enforce rule of law, all in servitude of the  empire. The convoy core transports raw material  between the mines and the city.  The dead are burned  as a tradition, no graves here.
Sunpetal Valley (Pop. 8-12k)

Haybrook is a small town five days' travel from  Halcyon. Sitting in the bottom of a mountain pass, this  sleepy little town houses a base for the Apollyean  Army. The 9th Dwarf is the local watering hole. Rowdy  and quaint, the 9th Dwarf primarily supplies bread,  soup, and ale, and is famous for their breadbowls.  There is a temple of Storms and Harvest, led by the  twin priests Ulder and Parkis, two human men who  have lived in Haybrook their entire lives. The military  base within Haybrook houses The Bastion of Defense,  an object said to protect the Apollyean Empire. It is a  common, unspoken belief that the Bastion is not  physical, but instead the idea of the Bastion is in and of  itself the Defender of the Empire.
Emerald Imperium (Pop 40k)

Sunpetal Valley is the nesting ground of many followers  of Daegher, Haerda, Dere, Idurh. Here, people get  together to celebrate festivals, share stories and  traditions, and for many, take a well-deserved vacation.  The scent of freshly baked bread wafts from bustling  bakeries, a perfect compliment to the abundance of  sunflowers, where the town gets its name. Artisans set  up shop to  display and sell their wares. Laughter is the  constant soundtrack of the city, echoing from public  squares where festivities occur nearly year-round, to  the lively chatter of friends sharing a meal at a cafe. Oil  lamps, intricately designed and glowing with a soft,  inviting light, illuminate the evenings, casting light and  warmth over the joyous celebrations that extend well  into the night.

Life in Sunpetal Valley moves at a gentle, unhurried  pace. The citizens, a testament to the city's benevolent  atmosphere, are known for their kindness, generosity,  and unwavering optimism. Children play freely in the
A marvel to behold, but built on the backs of over a  century’s worth of manual labour. The Emerald

many parks, while adults gather to share stories, skills,  and songs. It’s a place where every day feels like a  celebration ,and where the sun always seems to shine a  little brighter. I sure hope it isn’t a cult. Or secretly a  magic circle.
storms, fire, lightning — now, it is lost.
The Verdant Aegis

Within a dense, ever-shifting grove lies the Aegis, the  nexus of Enchanters. Students within the Aegis learnt of  how to charm and manipulate, how to pivot mortal  enemies into the best of allies, and how to sway  emotion on command.
Another City or Town we may Visit
Notable Geographical Features
The El’drath’el Range
The Luminary Spire

Atop Wraidorian there is said to be a gleaming white  Spire whose crystal lenses focus the very fabric of fate.  It is said Diviners practice prophecy, fate, and the future  once flocked here to commune with the stars, seek  omens, or even peer into possible futures.
Another cool thing
Wisdom of Ancients
The Infernal Sanctum
Throughout the world there were nine colleges,  though their locations have since been lost to time.

It is said that in a rip between that which is and that  which isn’t lies the Sanctum. Within the halls, Conjurers  once gathered to call forth the magic of the planes,  practicing summoning rituals precise enough to bend  reality.
Last Known Colleges & Locations
The Horizon Hall
The Arcanum of Astral Nexus
A place where those who seek knowledge bereft of  magic may gather to study. Perched at the edge of the  world, here scholars delve into philosophy, history,  diplomacy, and the art of war.
No one knows what befell the fortress of the Abjurers.  What is known is that within those walls, students  would gather to fabricate impervious defenses, both  magical and physical.
The Alcritous Forge

Deep within a mountain  said to be forged from  pure iron lies the Forge,  the College of  Transmutation. It is said  that here, transmitters  learn to reshape the fabric  of reality: turning lead to  gold, stone to flesh, and  fire into water.
The Chimeric Dominion
It is said that the Dominion lies within a city beyond  mortal reach, wherein Necromancers are taught the  cycles of both death and life, and manipulations of the  soul.
The Tenebrous Hollow

Said to be located beneath the roots of an ancient  forest lies the subterranean labyrinth known as the  Hollow. Here, Illusionists train to shape what the mind  perceives, to shape and create realities, and how to  stretch the boundaries of sight and thought.
The Tempest Citadel
Atop the highest peak  in the mortal realm, it is  said the Citadel lies  dormant. Once, great  Evokers honed the raw  forces of nature —

Tal visits Haybrook after the desecration of his family
Appendix
confused. Helwy is known as the God of  travellers, the dark, and sadness.
●​ Followers of Helwy tend to be neither good  nor evil explicitly, and often are searching for  something.
A.​ Pantheon
●​ Helwy is depicted as an owl with crescent  moon eyes.
Each member of the Wraidorian pantheon will be  depicted as any number of races, depending on the  location of the depiction. Oftentimes, multiple  depictions will be contained within the same space.
●​ Known as The Lost Child.
Each deity is also referred to by similar - though often  not the same - name. Below are the names typical of  Halcyon, alphabetically.
Illiard, Celestial of the Stars
●​ Illiard is not depicted very often, though when  he is, he is depicted as a scholar-type figure.  Illiard is the patron of hope, of dreams, of  sleep, and of that which is forgotten.
Major Deities
●​ Followers of Illiard tend to be neutral aligned,  though followers may interpret his teachings  however they choose.
Baerwu, God of the Sky

●​ Baerwiu is classically depicted as a wingless  dragon, though his color and design do tend to  vary based upon depiction. Baerwiu is thought  to lord over the domains of wind, indecision,  expanses, and heights.
●​ Illiard is symbolized by the interconnected  Ovis constellation, or the sheep.
●​ Known as The Drifter.
●​ Followers of Baerwiu tend to be chaotic in  nature.
Munder, God of Knowledge
●​ Baerwiu is symbolized as a wingless dragon.
●​ Munder is always depicted as an elderly,  greying, bearded, and kind human man. He is  known to lord over the domains of history,  alchemy, science, and truth.
●​ Known as The Above.
Ceolswy, Caretaker of Magic
●​ Munder is typically good-aligned, as  knowledge is virtuous in Wraidorian. To  followers of Munder, a good truth is worth its  weight in gold.
●​ After the collapse, all depictions of Ceolswy  have ceased to exist.
●​ Ceolswy is known as the Great Mage, or the  Fractured Mage, or the Arcane Architect
●​ Munder is symbolized as an open book, with a  large eye in the centerfold.
Daegher, Goddess of the Harvest
●​ Known as The Father.
●​ Daegher is the heart of the fields. Depicted as a  beautiful woman with skin and hair the color of  hay in the wind.
Ridoron, God of War
●​ Ridoran is depicted as a male figure, helmeted,  but without armor. He is used to symbolize  strategy, games, bloodshed, and conquest.
●​ She is also used to symbolize growth,  dedication, and workmanship. Daegher is also  the matron of the farm.
●​ Followers of Aethboed may be be of any  alignment, though most tend toward Evil.
●​ The symbol of Daegher is a hen with hay.
●​ She is known as the Golden Field.
●​ Symbolized as a crossed sword and flag
Ehlsik, God of Storms
●​ Known as the Great Conqueror.
●​ Elhlsik is typically depicted as a djinn-figure,  and can be used to symbolize change, hope,  and fear.
Witio, Goddess of the Earth
●​ Witio is always depicted as a dryad with  Wraidorian in her grasp. Witio largely rules  over the domains of nature, randomness and  symmetry.
●​ Typically understood to be a chaotic-aligned  deity, aligning closely with Chaotic Neutral.
●​ The symbol of Ehlsik is the sun shining over a  storming cloud.
●​ Witio is symbolized as a flowering tree, as  Wraidorian will always provide.
●​ Also known as the Storm Lord.
●​ Known as The Mother.
Helwy, Goddess of the Moon
●​ Helwy is typically depicted as a young man,  ruddy in completion, clean shaven, bald, and
Minor Deities
●​ Symbolized as an executioner's veil and scythe.
Aelwoth, God of Water
●​ Known as The Unknowable
●​ Commonly depicted as a young man with wild  hair and sunburnt skin, Aelwoth is used to  symbolize persistence, reflection, and  impulsiveness.
Haerda, Goddess of Life & Birth
●​ Haerda is depicted as an older woman, gentle  in her age. She is the matron of family, of all life  on Wraidorian, and fire.
●​ Aelwoth is typically neutral aligned as erosion  is ever present, and not considerate of  humanity.
●​ Haerda is typically a good-aligned deity, as are  her followers.
●​ Symbolized as a lit flame
●​ Symbolized as a reflection in a wave.
●​ Known as the Caretaker
●​ He is known as the Ever-Wave.
Idurh, Celestial of the Sun
Altent, God of Punishment
●​ Idurh is commonly depicted as a kind farmer  wearing a sun hat. Idurh lords over the day  cycle, warmth, kindness, and happiness.
●​ Altent is depicted as an executioner. It is said  that Altent lords over the domains of justice,  confinement, and slaughter.
●​ Followers of Idurh tend to be good-aligned.
●​ Altent is largely an evil-aligned deity, though  Lawful followers of Altent are not exceedingly  rare.
●​ Idurh is symbolized as the sun rising over  shaking hands.
●​ Known as the Friend to All.
●​ Altent is symbolized as the Winged Axe, as it is  said that all who face his wrath may attempt to  outrun him, though none have succeeded thus  far.
●​ Known as The Fallen Blade.
Bealda, Goddess of Reward
●​ Bealda is depicted as a bedazzled woman of  inordinate gemstones and jewelry. Bealda is  the patron of materialism, greed,  perseverance, and success.
●​ Due to the varying nature of being rewarded,  followers of Bealda may be of any alignment, as  long as they are rewarded for their services.
●​ Bealda is not explicitly related to a symbol, but  rather her followers ornament themselves in  vast wealth.
●​ She is known as The Temptress.
Dere, Goddess of Love
●​ Dere is depicted as a beautiful woman, her  visage ever-shifting. Dere also rules over  kinship, friendship, and passion.
●​ Followers of Dere are typically good-aligned,  or chaotic-aligned, as love is the chaotic  unification of souls.
●​ Symbolized by a gemstone.
●​ Known as the Fatespinner
Eawin, Entity of Death
●​ Eawin is depicted as a veiled figure, always  silent in nature. They are also used to  symbolize the transition between life and  death, the afterlife, and the unknown.
●​ Followers of Eawin are typically neutral in  alignment.
B.​ Tal Bawa’s
students and 5 elders. Closed off to most. Built high up  in the mountains, where the air was thin.
Village: Demsters
All students were taught by Master Gaoi, who taught  the students to let go from their earthly attachments,  and hone their bodies to be tools for the greater good.
The Demsters Burning
Overview

While Tal was away training in the mountains.  Demsters were attacked, there were no records of  what happened on that day, the entire town was razed.  Bodies of the dead had been piled and burned. No  evidence left but the remains of burnt arrows and the  trails of unknown beasts. No one was identifiable  except for old man Duncan who had been strung up in  such a way that his suffering was prolonged,  presumably to witness the destruction of his village.  The word of Demsters being burned traveled fast as  travelers hoping to try Bawa’s Ramen came to visit days  after, but only managed to see the grim sight.

A small and quaint farming town, specifically a  farming town that produces very high quality wheat.  Which had a population of 80, everyone knew  everyone. The town's nature has always been welcome,  most people who live there stumble upon it and decide  to settle. From all walks of life, veterans who left the  fields of battles, dwarfs who quit the mines, prisoners  looking for a clean slate, all creatures of all race,  ethnicity, and gender had been invited for a night or a  lifetime since the day the village first started.

The village was run by old man Duncan, the man who  with his friends, founded this town in the middle of  nowhere, with a charter from a noble of the Apollyean  to build a farm land. Originally he and his 5 friends  were the counsel, but as they all passed to time, they  had each chosen successors to take their place as was  their decided tradition. Everyone had a voice, town hall  meetings were monthly and productive. It was a little  slice of bureaucratic heaven.
C.​ Jan’s Farm
(Compound)
Careful-Hand Farmstead
Along  with the wheat, this village also grew and  raised most of what it needed to survive, with  additional bi weekly imports from other villages or  cities.

Located to the south of Halycon, the Careful-Hand  Farmstead is a farm-turned-rehabilitation compound  for low-risk offenders to learn practical skills,  discipline, and earn stable income to get themselves  back on their feet. With the goal to produce  contributing and productive members of society, it is  run by the paladin of Daegher, Jan, and his familiar cat,  Cheeto.
It was a happy town where everyone had enough,  funding from the occasional tourism and exports  allowed for the town to flourish.

The weather changes between hot summers and cold  winters, it's a middle point between multiple larger  towns and due to its large crop yield and even more  importantly, its fantastic ramen shop, it was well known  throughout the region.
The farmland is generational land tilled by servants  of Daegher, but only recently in Jan’s generation has it  started to adopt its second purpose as a rehabilitation  center.
Bawa’s Ramen

Typically hosting nine  convicts as farmhands  (opportunity presents itself to grant a new life), the  farm produces barley and apples and in the winter  doubles as a carpentry workshop producing chairs and  tables.
A small ramen shop in seemingly the middle of  nowhere, yet nearly everyone has heard of it, many  travel from afar just to try the famous Bawa Ramen.

Started by Merlo Bawa, or Mer as he was known  around town. He was  a quiet man who stumbled upon  this town. No one knew his backstory, and he was quite  reserved, but what the people did know was his  cooking was good, and with the proper ingredients his  Ramen was surely the best.
“A simple life is a disciplined life.” This is definitely not  the plot of Holes because I don’t remember anything  about that movie.
He soon met a woman and had a son, Tal Bawa, but  shortly after he was born the mother left town  suddenly.
Soryuu Monastary
A monastery located 48km, or a day's journey away  from Demsters, where Tal ventured off to train in the  ways of being a monk. A small monastery with about 20
